title: Sterigmostemum matthioloides
taxonomy: Sterigmostemum matthioloides is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. It is part of the tribe Gnaphalieae, a group of plants that are often found in dry and rocky areas. The genus Sterigmostemum is a small group of plants that are primarily found in Africa, with Sterigmostemum matthioloides being one of the species within this group. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and the characteristics of its flowers and leaves. Further research may be needed to fully understand the relationships between Sterigmostemum matthioloides and other plants within the Asteraceae family.
uses: Sterigmostemum matthioloides, a plant of interest, has been used in traditional medicine.
